Name: Elasticsearch End of Life (EOL) Detection
description: The Elasticsearch version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.
detection_result: The "Elasticsearch" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
detection_method: Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.
Details: Elasticsearch End of Life (EOL) Detection
impact: An EOL version of Elasticsearch is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unxed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.
solution: Update Elasticsearch to a version that still receives technical support and updates.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch 'CVE-2018-3831' Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux
description: Elasticsearch is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch 'CVE-2018-3831' Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Li.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an authenticated attacker to acquire valid login credentials.
insight: The _cluster/settings API, when queried, could leak sensitive conguration information such as passwords, tokens or usernames.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch Security < 5.6.15 / 6.x < 6.6.1 Permission Issue (ESA-2019-04)
description: Elasticsearch Security is prone to a permission issue.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch Security < 5.6.15 / 6.x < 6.6.1 Permission Issue (ESA-201.
impact: This aw could result in an attacker gaining additional permissions against a restricted index.
solution: Users unable to update can change the xpack.security.dls_s.enabled setting to true in their elasticsearch.yml le. The default setting for this option is true.
insight: A permission issue was found in Elasticsearch when Field Level Security and Document Level Security are disabled and the _aliases, _shrink, or _split endpoints are used. If the elasticsearch.yml le has xpack.security.dls_s.enabled set to false, certain permission checks are skipped when users perform one of the actions mentioned above, to make existing data available under a new index/alias name.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.3.0 Information Exposure Vulnerability (ESA-2018-11)
description: Elasticsearch is prone to an information exposure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.3.0 Information Exposure Vulnerability (ESA-2018-11)
impact: When the repository-azure plugin is set to log at TRACE level Azure credentials can be inadvertently logged.
insight: A sensitive data disclosure aw was found in the Elasticsearch repository-azure (formerly elasticsearch-cloud-azure) plugin.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.8.12, 7.x < 7.9.0 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux
description: Elasticsearch is prone to a eld disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.8.12, 7.x < 7.9.0 Information Disclosure Vulnerabilit.  ->..
impact: An attacker could gain additional permissions against a restricted index.
insight: A eld disclosure aw was found in Elasticsearch when running a scrolling search with Field  Level Security. If a user runs the same query another more privileged user recently ran, the  scrolling search can leak elds that should be hidden.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch DoS Vulnerability (ESA-2021-15)
description: Elasticsearch is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: An uncontrolled recursion vulnerability that could lead to a denial of service attack was iden-  tied in the Elasticsearch Grok parser. A user with the ability to submit arbitrary queries to  Elasticsearch could create a malicious Grok query that will crash the Elasticsearch node.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.8.2, 7.x < 7.2.1 Information Disclosure Vulnerability (ESA-2019-  07) - Linux
description: Elasticsearch is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch < 6.8.2, 7.x < 7.2.1 Information Disclosure Vulnerability.  ->..
impact: On a system with multiple users submitting requests, it could be possible for an attacker to gain  access to response header containing sensitive data from another user.
insight: A race condition aw was found in the response headers Elasticsearch returns to a request.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch Multiple Vulnerabilities (ESA-2021-06, ESA-2021-08)
description: Elasticsearch is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch Multiple Vulnerabilities (ESA-2021-06, ESA-2021-08)
impact: This could lead to disclosing the existence of documents and elds the attacker should not be  able to view or result in an attacker gaining additional insight into potentially sensitive indices.
insight: - CVE-2021-22135: Suggester & Prole API information disclosure aw

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch Information Disclosure Vulnerability (ESA-2021-03)
description: Elasticsearch is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch Information Disclosure Vulnerability (ESA-2021-03) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.145383
impact: This could allow an Elasticsearch administrator to view sensitive details.
insight: Elasticsearch has an information disclosure issue when audit logging and the emit_request_body option is enabled. The Elasticsearch audit log could contain sensitive information such as password hashes or authentication tokens.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 3559475662 Packet 2: 3559476774
impact: A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled' Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'

Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.  The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options  when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication  includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.   See the references for more information.   Aected Software/OS   TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch Information Disclosure Vulnerability (ESA-2020-13)
description: Elasticsearch is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Elastic Elasticsearch Information Disclosure Vulnerability (ESA-2020-13) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.117181
impact: This could result in the search disclosing the existence of documents the attacker should not be able to view. This could result in an attacker gaining additional insight into potentially sensitive indices.
solution: Aected Software/OS Elasticsearch versions before 6.8.13 and 7.x before 7.9.2.
insight: A document disclosure aw was found in Elasticsearch when Document or Field Level Security is used. Search queries do not properly preserve security permissions when executing certain complex queries.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.   Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:  After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen  that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with  the older CPE.   Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.  Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.
log_method: Details: Hostname Determination Reporting   OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.108449   Version used: 2022-07-27T10:11:28Z

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information
log_method: Details: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an an
->swer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.
Details: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.14.5):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: Elastic Elasticsearch Detection Consolidation
description: Consolidation of Elastic Elasticsearch detections.
detection_result: health status index pri rep docs.count docs.deleted store.size

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: A web server is running on this port
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
description: The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The congured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan cong in use

- The congured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan cong in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.

Name: Service Detection with 'GET' Request
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: An Elasticsearch Binary API / inter-cluster communication service seems to be running on this port.
insight: This plugin is a complement of the plugin 'Services' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10330). It sends a HTTP 'GET' request to the remaining unknown services and tries to identify them.
